# Obligatory Java to Python Migration Good Code Practices

Follow these rules in each migration batch:

1. Preserve behavior first (migrate/add tests before refactoring).
2. Split Java classes into focused Python modules.
3. Use Pythonic patterns (properties only when needed, avoid Java-style copying).
4. Keep type hints and dataclasses where suitable.
5. Convert checked-exception flows into clear Python exceptions.
6. Follow Python naming conventions (`snake_case`, `PascalCase`).
7. Re-check performance-sensitive paths after migration.
8. Run linting, tests, and static checks before moving to next batch.
